title: The Land of Eem - Character Creation and Book of Encounters (solo RPG)
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=RUy1hElRuUQ

[Summary]

Daniel (@dungeondive) walks through creating a character for The Land of Eem, starting with establishing your concept and moving through eight straightforward steps that demand no randomization—only choices. He creates Harlon Peters, a dungeoner from the desolate Used to be Forest, and pairs him with a loyal chum named Sammy. What's delightful here is how little of your fate is left to chance; you assign your four attributes (Vim, Vigor, Knack, Knowhow) and tweak skills with intention, then roll for your two starting mundane items and backstory beats. Daniel also showcases the Book of Encounters, an incredible D100 expansion that transforms travel checks into something genuinely exciting—each region gets its own encounter tables for perilous moments, dangers, and discoveries. The core rulebook offers D20 charts for these encounters, but the Book of Encounters expands them tenfold. Daniel demonstrates several encounters rolled live: a dworg arrow-victim potentially creating a side quest, hungry creatures dragging adventurers underground, a brook of chattering fairies offering rumors. He touches on his approach to solo play using oracle tools like his own 3d6 Oracle and Bag of Fate, plus Gustavo Quelho's GM Emulator—all layered beneath a game that fundamentally rewards exploration and discovery over combat.

----

When you're building a dungeoner (or any class in Eem), what draws you most—the freedom to define your attributes perfectly, or would you rather have a bit of randomization to surprise you?
